faq_1_question: What is Duty Drawback and how can it reduce my customs costs?
faq_1_answer: Duty Drawback is a process that allows you to recover <strong>100% of duties, taxes, and fees</strong> paid on imported goods that are later exported or destroyed. For retail brands, this means reclaiming costs on <strong>customer returns shipped back overseas</strong>. It directly improves your landed cost margins and cash flow.
faq_2_question: Can non-UK brands claim Duty Drawback on returned goods exported from the UK?
faq_2_answer: <strong>Yes</strong>, if you meet three conditions: (1) You were the <strong>importer</strong> when the goods entered the UK, (2) documentation show that returned goods <strong>identical</strong> to the imported ones, and (3) the return transaction took place within <strong>3 years</strong> of claim submission. Many non-UK brands overlook this. Returns can generate <strong>6-8% cost savings</strong> on original import duties.
faq_3_question: Can UK brands exporting inventory that was previously imported benefit from duty free solutions?
faq_3_answer: <p>If you import goods into the UK and later export them <strong>unused</strong> to consignees (e.g., distributors, subsidiaries), you can avoid paying for import duties. <strong>UK bonded warehouse</strong>&nbsp;is the&nbsp;most practical solution for retail.</p>
faq_4_question: What documentation is critical for Duty Drawback claims?
faq_4_answer: <p>Simply put, <strong>data and documentation</strong> that relate to <strong>import and export processes</strong> are required. That includes commercial invoice and also customs entry details.<br /><br /><strong>The TDR Advantage:</strong> Our team will provide all necessary support to create a compliant repository of documentation. With your consent, they will also access your&nbsp;<strong>CDS&nbsp;portal</strong>&nbsp;and pull down necessary data.</p>
faq_5_question: How long does it take to receive Duty Drawback refunds?
faq_5_answer: <p><strong>HMRC processes claims in 1-3 months</strong> on average.<br /><br /><strong>The TDR Advantage:</strong> Our support team submit claims and handles customs enquiry online to minimise processing time.<br /><br /><strong>Finance teams</strong>: Plan for a 3<strong>-month working capital cycle</strong>&mdash;but refunds are <strong>interest-free</strong>, so the ROI is effectively the full duty amount recovered.</p>
faq_6_question: What percentage of duties and fees can I recover?
faq_6_answer: <p><strong>100% of duties, taxes, and most fees</strong>. Claims must be submitted within 3 years after the initial importation date.</p>
faq_7_question: What are the top reasons Duty Drawback claims get rejected?
faq_7_answer: <p><strong>Avoid these mistakes</strong>: (1) <strong>Missing export proof&nbsp;</strong>(e.g., no CDS entry details, no commercial invoice...), (2) <strong>Inadequate inventory tracking</strong> (can't link imports to exports), (3) <strong>Late filings</strong> (3-year deadline from import), (4) <strong>Incorrect HTS classification</strong> mismatches, and (5) <strong>Using non-identical goods</strong>.<br /><br /><strong>The TDR Advantage:</strong> Our team will support you implementing a <strong>drawback-specific ERP workflow</strong> before claiming.</p>
faq_8_question: Should we file Duty Drawback claims in-house or use a customs broker?
faq_8_answer: <p><strong>DIY</strong>: Feasible if you have <strong>an HMRC approved customs software and robust customs expertise</strong>. <br /> Alternatively, <strong>TDR</strong> takes care of all the heavy lifting, the EDI connection with Customs and typically increases recovery rate by 30% through optimization.</p>
faq_9_question: Are there audit risks with Duty Drawback, and how can we mitigate them?
faq_9_answer: <p><strong>Yes</strong>—HMRC audits <strong>~5% of claims</strong> annually. <strong>Red flags</strong>: High claim volumes, frequent substitution use, or inconsistent documentation. <strong>Mitigation</strong>: (1) <strong>Conduct a pre-filing audit</strong> of your records, (2) <strong>Use CDS platform</strong> (HMRC's preferred system), (3) <strong>Document everything</strong> (e.g., why substitution was used), and (4) <strong>Designate a single POC</strong> for HMRC inquiries. <strong>Penalties</strong> for negligence can include <strong>denied claims</strong> or <strong>fines up to the duty amount</strong>.<br><br><strong>The TDR Advantage:</strong> Trust our expert team to submit fully compliant claims to HMRC</strong> and reduce the risk of Customs audits.</p>
faq_10_question: Why should I choose TDR as my drawback partner?
faq_10_answer: <p><strong>Dedicated retail duty drawback expertise:&nbsp;</strong>TDR is the only partner focused on crossborder retail drawback, with a specialist process built for eCommerce returns and re-exported inventory.</p><p><strong>Cost-effective for any retailer:&nbsp;</strong>No budget threshold and success-based fees mean TDR supports small and large drawback budgets, so retailers pay only when recovery succeeds.</p><p><strong>Simplifies global duty recovery:&nbsp;</strong>One partner handles UK, EU, and UK duty mitigation without the customer needing a separate coordinator, reducing complexity and improving cash flow.</p>
